ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source (*.hs) of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). You MAY: run the reference binary/servers via your cluster's oracle scripts (export PATH="$PATH:/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin" if invoking directly — maude/dot live there), read .spthy example files, and read/write freely inside your own cluster directory under /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/. Do not modify anything outside your cluster directory. Do not run git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction (command + salient output) by appending to your cluster's workspace QUERIES.log; record every derived behavioral fact in BEHAVIOR.md with provenance (which probe established it); write your round summary into a REPORT file in the workspace (append a new round section). Code comments must describe current behavior only — no narration of changes or process. If your model's training data tempts you to "recall" how tamarin's internals work, do not use it: every behavioral claim must trace to a logged probe or a captured fixture. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary at the end (no structured output).

UNIT C (wellformedness), round 4 — close the two gaps that keep the crate from fully replacing post-elaboration checking.
Cluster: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/ — read the workspace BEHAVIOR.md, QUERIES.log, SPEC*.md and prior REPORTs first; the oracle wrapper script and its usage are documented there (it accepts extra args such as --diff). Crate: workspace/wf-clean.

GAP 1 — sort-aware quantifier binding in the wrong-form-terms check. Captured reference behavior you currently fail to reproduce: on a theory containing
    lemma L: "All #x. (K(x) @ #x) ==> F"
the reference prints:  Lemma `L' uses terms of the wrong form: `Free x'
That is: the temporal binder #x does NOT bind the message-position use x. Your current name-only de-Bruijn binding wrongly binds it and reports nothing (a silent false negative). Systematically probe how quantifier binding interacts with variable sort annotations: #x vs plain x, x:pub, x:fresh, x:nat, same-name binders of different sorts in both directions, nested and shadowed quantifiers, uses in temporal position vs message position. Rework the binding model so every probe matches the oracle byte-exactly. Add fixtures for each probe family.

GAP 2 — guardedness must be decided semantically. Your current guardedness classification is a documented heuristic over-approximation. Probe the reference's guardedness warning exhaustively: existential failure sub-modes, exotic universal bodies, nested/mixed quantifiers, which atom kinds may appear as guards, negation placement, conjunction/disjunction/implication in bodies, quantified variables not covered by the guard atoms, multiple guard atoms, vacuous quantifiers. Also probe which theory item kinds can produce this warning at all (lemmas only? others?). Replace the heuristic with a decision procedure matching the oracle on the full probe set, with byte-exact report text (including the formula rendering your round-3 printer produces). Add fixtures.

All existing tests and fixtures must stay green; run the full fixture suite at the end.

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1	# Clean-room task: wellformedness checker
2	
3	Build a standalone Rust crate `wf-clean` in workspace/ that reimplements,
4	from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, the wellformedness checking of the
5	tamarin-prover binary (the oracle).
6	
7	## What the oracle does
8	`oracle/wf_oracle.sh <file.spthy>` prints the tool's full output for a
9	security-protocol theory file. Within that output, wellformedness checking
10	produces either `/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */` or a
11	WARNING section listing check failures grouped under underlined topic
12	headers. Your job: given a parsed theory (data model in
13	interface/ast_types.rs), produce byte-identical report text and the
14	structured API in interface/required_api.md.
15	
16	## Method requirements
17	- Derive the set of checks, their trigger conditions, their exact message
18	  strings, their grouping and ORDER purely by experimenting against the
19	  oracle: write minimal .spthy inputs that trip each check one at a time.
20	  37 pre-captured outputs are in oracle/captures/; example inputs (usable as
21	  starting points for mutations) in oracle/examples/. The .spthy language
22	  syntax may be learned from those examples and the oracle's parse errors.
23	- Log every oracle query's purpose in workspace/QUERIES.log (one line each).
24	- Maintain workspace/BEHAVIOR.md: the growing behavioral spec you infer
25	  (check name, trigger, message template, ordering). This document is a
26	  deliverable equal in importance to the code.
27	- Tests: for each check, a fixture .spthy + expected report snippet, asserted
28	  against your implementation AND spot-verified against the oracle.
29	- Dependencies: std only. You may NOT parse .spthy yourself; your crate's
30	  input is the ast_types.rs data model (write test values directly as Rust
31	  constructors; oracle fixtures verify message/ordering behavior).
32

1	# Round 3 (Unit C): complete the wellformedness surface
2	
3	Extend workspace/wf-clean. Oracle: oracle/wf_oracle.sh <file> [flags].
4	1. "Formula terms" FULL coverage: the oracle flags formula terms containing
5	   reducible user-defined functions (theories with `equations:`). Your new
6	   entry point takes the theory PLUS a caller-supplied set of reducible
7	   function symbol names (the caller computes it; you only consume it).
8	   Probe with user-equation theories; byte-match messages incl. the de Bruijn
9	   `Bound N` renderings you noted in round 1.
10	2. Formula guardedness: close the round-1 gaps (multi-line wrapped formula
11	   rendering; precise nested-subformula selection) by probing formulas the
12	   oracle wraps across indented lines.
13	3. Probe whether a "Fact capitalization issues" topic exists (hint: fact
14	   names differing only in case across rules) and implement byte-exact.
15	4. Close remaining round-1/2 documented gaps that are AST-derivable:
16	   fsep wrapping of wide fact lists (~col 80), fresh-const list wrapping,
17	   lemma-fact arity conflicts, multi-group public-names separators.
18	OUT OF SCOPE: Message Derivation / Derivation Checks (separate unit).
19	Update BEHAVIOR.md/QUERIES.log; tests per item; REPORT3.md + 10-line summary.

1	# Behavioral spec inferred from the oracle (wellformedness checker)
2	
3	All facts below are derived from oracle observation (probes in
4	scratchpad/probes, captures in oracle/captures). Nothing here is taken from
5	tamarin-prover source.
6	
7	## Overall report shape
8	
9	The oracle emits the wellformedness result inside the pretty-printed theory,
10	wrapped in a block comment.
11	
12	Success (empty report):
13	
14	    /* All wellformedness checks were successful. */
15	
16	Failure (>=1 topic):
17	
18	    /*
19	    WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!
20	
21	    <block1>
22	
23	    <block2>
24	    ...
25	    */
26	
27	Inner text (what `render_report` produces for a non-empty report) is:
28	
29	    "WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!" + "\n\n" + join(blocks, "\n\n")
30	
31	Each `block` = topic header + "\n" + underline + "\n\n" + body, where
32	`underline` = '=' repeated char_count(topic) (topic length INCLUDING any
33	leading/trailing space). Bodies may themselves contain blank lines.
34	
35	`render_report` for the empty report returns the success line
36	`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`.
37	`underline_topic(t)` returns `t + "\n" + "=".repeat(char_count(t))`.
38	
39	## Canonical topic order (report order)
40	
41	Observed by kitchen-sink probes ks1 / ks3 and the round-2 ordering probes
42	(comb1, ord_tail, mega_tail, diff_mega, diff_place):
43	
44	 1. `Unbound variables`
45	 2. `Fresh public constants`                             (round2; always-on)
46	 3. `Public constants with mismatching capitalization`   (public-names report)
47	 4. `Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization`
48	 5. `Reserved names`
49	 5a. `Reserved prefixes`                                 (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
50	 6. `Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable`
51	 7. `Special facts`
52	 7b. `Fact capitalization issues`                        (round3; before arity)
53	 8. `Fact arity issues`                                  (non-diff; diff merges these
54	 9. `Fact multiplicity issues`                            two into "Fact usage" - OUT OF SCOPE)
55	10. `Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side ` (TRAILING space)
56	10a. `Left rule` / `Right rule`                          (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
57	11. `Formula terms`
58	12. ` Formula guardedness`   (LEADING space)
59	13. `Lemma annotations`                                  (round2; always-on)
60	14. `Multiplication restriction of rules`                (round2; always-on)
61	15. `Nat Sorts`
62	16. `Subterm Convergence Warning`
63	17. `Message Derivation Checks` (appended by binary; needs Maude - OUT OF SCOPE)
64	    / `Derivation Checks` (timeout variant - OUT OF SCOPE)
65	
66	Round-2 ordering evidence: comb1 -> `Unbound, Fresh public constants,
67	Public names, mismatching sorts, Reserved`; mega_tail -> `Formula terms,
68	Formula guardedness, Lemma annotations, Multiplication restriction, Nat Sorts,
69	Subterm`; diff_mega/diff_place -> `... Reserved names, Reserved prefixes,
70	Fr facts, ... lhs-not-rhs, Left/Right rule, Formula terms, ...`.
71	
72	Public names (#3) is generated separately and *inserted before* the first of a
73	set of anchor topics = every topic from #4 (mismatching sorts) onward.
74	`after_public_names_topics()` returns that anchor list; `insert_wf_before(
75	report, errs, anchors)` inserts `errs` immediately before the first report
76	entry whose topic is in `anchors` (appends at end if none present). Unbound
77	variables (#1) and Fresh public constants (#2) are emitted before the
78	public-names insertion point (so they are NOT anchors).
79	
80	### Diff-mode-only vs. always-on
81	
82	Gated on `theory.is_diff`: `Reserved prefixes`, `Left rule`, `Right rule`
83	(observed silent in non-diff mode; diff_reserved_prefix without `--diff` emits
84	nothing). Always-on (both modes): `Fresh public constants`, `Lemma
85	annotations`, `Multiplication restriction of rules`.
86	
87	## Per-check triggers and message templates
88	
89	### Unbound variables
90	Trigger: a protocol rule uses a variable in actions/conclusions not bound by a
91	premise. Entries joined by "\n  \n":
92	    "  rule `NAME' has unbound variables: \n    v1, v2"
93	(trailing space after "variables:"). Vars sorted, ", " joined, term-printed.
94	
95	### Public constants with mismatching capitalization (public_names_report)
96	Trigger: two public constants whose names are equal under ASCII-lowercasing but
97	differ in capitalization. Body:
98	    "Identifiers are case-sensitive, mismatched capitalizations are considered as different, i.e., 'ID' is different from 'id'. Check the capitalization of your identifiers.\n\n" + numbered items
99	Each lowercased-key group -> one item "  N. " + occurrences. Occurrences group by
100	location; same loc: `rule "R1":  name 'Alice', 'alice'`; different locs joined
101	", ": `rule "R1":  name 'Server', rule "R2":  name 'server'`. Names ASCII-sorted.
102	`public_names_report_from_pairs` takes raw (location, name) pairs.
103	
104	### Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization
105	Trigger: within one rule, two variables share a base name but differ in sort
106	prefix ($x/x/~x) or capitalization. Fixed header:
107	    "Possible reasons:\n1. Identifiers are case sensitive, i.e.,'x' and 'X' are considered to be different.\n2. The same holds for sorts:, i.e., '$x', 'x', and '~x' are considered to be different.\n"
108	per rule: "  rule `A5': \n    1. $s, ~s" (trailing space; numbered variant groups).
109	
110	### Reserved names
111	Trigger: rule uses a fact with a reserved name in a position. The reserved set
112	is POSITION-DEPENDENT (observed z9/z11/z12):
113	  - left-hand-side / right-hand-side: {K, KU, KD}
114	  - the middle (actions): {K, KU, KD, In, Out, Fr}  (the I/O facts are also
115	    reserved as actions; on LHS/RHS they are "Special facts" instead)
116	Per (rule, position) entry joined "\n  \n":
117	    "  Rule `R1' contains facts with reserved names on left-hand-side:\n    <facts>"
118	Position phrases: LHS `on left-hand-side:`, actions `on the middle:`,
119	RHS `on the right-hand-side:`. Facts via fact printer, fsep comma-joined.
120	
121	### Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable
122	Trigger: `Fr(t)` where t is not fresh/msg var. Entries joined "\n  \n":
123	    "  rule `R1' fact: Fr( $p )"
124	
125	### Special facts
126	Trigger: reserved I/O fact in disallowed position (Out/Fr/K in premises,
127	In/K in conclusions). Per (rule, side) joined "\n  \n":
128	    "  rule `R1' uses disallowed facts on left-hand-side:\n    Out( x )"
129	(sides `on left-hand-side:`/`on right-hand-side:`; lowercase "rule").
130	
131	### Fact arity issues
132	Trigger: same fact name with >1 arity. Body:
133	    "Same fact is used with different arities, i.e., Fact('A','B') is different from Fact('A'). \nCheck the arguments of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
134	facts sorted by lowercased name; factBlock (each prefixed "\n"):
135	    "\n  Fact `foo':\n\n" + items + "\n  "
136	item i: "    {i}. {Label} `{owner}', arity {n}\n         {render}"; items joined
137	"\n    \n". Rules and LEMMAS both contribute (interleaved in theory-item order);
138	restrictions do NOT (r3_restrarity). Rule facts: Label=`Rule`, render=fact pp.
139	Lemma action facts: Label=`Lemma`, render = raw Haskell `Fact {factTag =
140	ProtoFact {Linear|Persistent} "{name}" {arity}, factAnnotations = fromList [],
141	factTerms = [{de-Bruijn terms}]}` (r3_lemarity). Rule items dedup per
142	(label,owner,arity); lemma facts likewise. Multiplicity is analogous and ALSO
143	gathers lemma facts (r3_lemmult, item = `... multiplicity (persistence) ...`).
144	
145	### Fact multiplicity issues
146	Same structure keyed on multiplicity; header:
147	    "Same fact is used with different multiplicities, i.e., !Fact() (Persistent fact) exists along with Fact() (Linear) in your rules. \nCheck the multiplicity (persistence) of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
148	item i: "    {i}. {Label} `{owner}', multiplicity (persistence) {Linear|Persistent}\n         {render}".
149	
150	### Fact capitalization issues (round3; precedes Fact arity issues)
151	Trigger: two facts whose names are equal under ASCII-lowercasing but differ in
152	exact spelling (e.g. `Send` vs `SEND`; facts must start upper-case, so the
153	difference is in non-first letters). Same block shape as Fact arity, header:
154	    "Fact names are case-sensitive, different capitalizations are considered as different facts, i.e., Fact() is different from FAct(). \nCheck the capitalization of your fact names.\n  " + factBlocks
155	Unlike arity, EVERY occurrence is listed (NO per-(rule,cap) dedup - `Send` twice
156	in one rule -> two items; r3_capord). item i:
157	    "    {i}. Rule `{rule}', capitalization \"{exactName}\"\n         {factPP}".
158	Groups keyed by lowercased name, sorted; a group needs >=2 distinct spellings.
159	Gathers rule facts across premises/actions/conclusions (source order).
160	
161	### Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side  (fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs)
162	Trigger: user fact identity (name,arity,mult) in some premise, never in any
163	conclusion. Numbered items joined "\n  \n":
164	    "  1. in rule \"r1\":  factName `X' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear" +
165	      optional ". Perhaps you want to use the fact in rule \"r1\":  factName `Y' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear"
166	suggestion = nearest RHS fact by edit distance under a threshold (omitted when
167	nothing close). Special facts excluded from both sides.
168	
169	### Fresh public constants (round2; always-on)
170	Trigger: a fresh-name literal (`~'foo'`, AST `Term::FreshLit`) used directly in
171	a rule. Constants collected in the order premises, conclusions, actions
172	(probe fpc_positions). Per rule with >=1 hit, entries joined "\n  \n":
173	    "  rule `NAME': fresh public constants are not allowed: ~'foo', ~'bar'"
174	constants rendered by the term printer. The list is a fillSep at width 69 with a
175	4-space continuation indent, begun after the header `  rule `{name}': fresh
176	public constants are not allowed:` (round3: r3_freshwrap; identical mechanism to
177	the Formula-terms list). CLOSED - was a documented gap.
178	
179	### Reserved prefixes (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
180	Trigger (diff theories only): a fact whose name starts with `DiffIntr` or
181	`DiffProto`. Facts collected in the order premises, actions, conclusions
182	(probe rp_multi). Per rule the body is:
183	    "  The Rule `NAME' contains facts with reserved prefixes ('DiffIntr',\n  'DiffProto') inside names:\n  \n" + factblocks
184	The HEADER is word-filled (Wadler `fillSep`) at WIDTH 69, indent 2 (measured via
185	rp_long/rp_med/rp_w47: a line breaks before the next word once column would
186	exceed 69). Words treat the rule name `\`NAME'` and each of `('DiffIntr',` /
187	`'DiffProto')` as single tokens. Each factblock (joined "\n  \n"):
188	    "    <fact_pp>\n    (ProtoFact <Mult> \"<name>\" <arity>,<arity>,<Mult>)"
189	The 2nd line is a raw Haskell Show of a (FactTag, arity, Multiplicity) tuple;
190	<Mult> is `Linear`/`Persistent`. GAP: multi-RULE joining unprobed (fixtures use
191	one rule).
192	
193	### Left rule / Right rule (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
194	Trigger (diff theories only): a diff rule (`Rule.left_right = Some((l,r))`)
195	whose explicit `left`/`right` projection differs from the parent rule projected
196	to that side (`diff(a,b)` -> a on left, -> b on right). Per rule the LEFT
197	projection is tested first; if inconsistent the RIGHT is not reported for that
198	rule (probe diff_both: both sides bad -> only "Left rule"). Body per entry
199	(joined "\n  \n"):
200	    "  Inconsistent left rule\n" + indent4(explicit_rule_pp) +
201	    "\n  \n  w.r.t.\n  \n" + indent4(parent_rule_pp)
202	("left"/"right" and topic "Left rule"/"Right rule"). Consistency compared via
203	the projected vs. explicit fact lists. Left rule topic precedes Right rule.
204	
205	### Lemma annotations (round2; always-on)
206	Trigger: a lemma with attribute `reuse` AND quantifier `exists-trace` (reuse on
207	`all-traces` is fine - probe la_alltraces). Entries joined "\n  \n":
208	    "  Lemma `NAME': cannot reuse 'exists-trace' lemmas"
209	(Topic may host other annotation conflicts not yet observed.)
210	
211	### Multiplication restriction of rules (round2; always-on)
212	Trigger: a rule whose CONCLUSIONS contain a multiplication (`*`, AST
213	`BinOp::Mult`) term. `*` only in premises or only in actions does NOT trigger
214	(probes mul_multi, mul_act); a `*` in a premise is not listed even when the
215	conclusion also has one (mul_pc). Per rule, entry (joined "\n  \n"):
216	    "  The following rule is not multiplication restricted:\n" + indent4(rule_pp) +
217	    "\n  \n  After replacing reducible function symbols in lhs with variables:\n" + indent4(rule2_pp) +
218	    "\n  \n    Terms with multiplication:  <terms>"
219	<terms> = maximal `*`-subterms of the conclusions, ", " joined (mul_terms).
220	GAPS: (a) rule2 (LHS reducible symbols replaced by fresh vars) is rendered equal
221	to rule1 - correct only when the LHS has no reducible symbols (probe mul_exp
222	shows `g^c` -> `x.1` otherwise); (b) the alternate failure mode
223	"Variables that occur only in rhs:  <vars>" (mul_exp) is not implemented; (c)
224	the co-emitted `Message Derivation Checks` block is Maude-derived, OUT OF SCOPE;
225	(d) rule_pp wrapping for long rules (see printer note).
226	
227	### Formula terms  (round3: FULL coverage)
228	Trigger: a lemma/restriction formula uses a TERM "of the wrong form". The unit
229	checked is each ARGUMENT TERM of each atom (Eq/Less/.. -> both sides; Action ->
230	the TEMPORAL first, then the fact args; Pred/Last -> args). A term is wrong iff
231	it contains a FREE variable OR a REDUCIBLE function symbol; the WHOLE top-level
232	term is then reported (not the offending subterm). Body:
233	    "  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form: {termlist}\n  \n" + fixed_help
234	Entity = `Lemma` or `Restriction`. Terms are collected in source order and are
235	NOT deduplicated (`x=y & x=y` -> `Free y', `Free y'`). Reducibility is decided by
236	the CALLER, which supplies a set of reducible function-symbol names; the checker
237	only consumes it (entry point `formula_terms_reducible(thy, &reducible)`;
238	`formula_terms(thy)` = the empty-set convenience wrapper - free variables only).
239	
240	Raw term rendering (each term wrapped in `` `...' ``):
241	  - bound variable   -> `Bound N`   (de Bruijn: 0 = innermost binder; EVERY
242	                                      quantified var, incl. temporals, pushes one)
243	  - free variable    -> `Free <pp_var>`  (keeps the sort prefix, e.g. `Free #j`)
244	  - function app      -> `f(a,b)`   (args comma-joined, NO space)
245	  - tuple             -> `pair(a,pair(b,c))`  (right-nested binary pairs)
246	  - public constant   -> `'name'`
247	De Bruijn evidence: `All x y #i. h(x,y)` -> `h(Bound 2,Bound 1)`; nesting adds
248	depth (`All x #i.(Ex y #j. h(x))` -> `h(Bound 3)`). Free-inside-term evidence:
249	`f(y)` (f non-reducible, y free) -> `f(Free y)`; `f(h(x))` -> `f(h(Bound 1))`.
250	
251	Term-list layout is a fillSep at width 69 with a 4-space continuation indent,
252	begun after the header (`  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form:`): a
253	token (term + trailing comma, except the last) stays on the line while
254	`col + 1 + width <= 69`, else wraps. A long entity/name can push the entire list
255	to line 2; an over-wide single term also wraps to line 2. Multiple wrong lemmas:
256	each full block (header+list+help) is joined by `\n  \n`.
257	fixed_help = "  The only allowed terms are public constants and bound node and\n  message variables. If you encounter free message variables, then\n  you might have forgotten a #-prefix. Sort prefixes can only be\n  dropped where this is unambiguous. Moreover, reducible function\n  symbols are disallowed."
258	
259	### Formula guardedness (topic has LEADING space)  (round3: two modes + wrapping)
260	Trigger: a LEMMA formula not convertible to guarded form. (An unguarded
261	RESTRICTION is instead a FATAL error, not a warning -- observed z1; lemmas only.)
262	Body:
263	    "  Lemma `{name}' cannot be converted to a guarded formula:\n    {reason}\n      \"<sub>\"\n    in the formula\n      \"<whole>\""
264	where <sub> is the FAILING QUANTIFIER SUBTREE (not the whole formula) and <whole>
265	is the full lemma formula. Two observed reasons (probe r3_gc):
266	  - `unguarded variable(s) '#j', ... in the subformula`  (quantifier binds vars
267	    not guarded by an action fact)
268	  - `universal quantifier without toplevel implication`
269	Guardedness decision (∀): the body must be a top-level `guard ==> rest`; its
270	ANTECEDENT's action facts (temporal incl.) must bind every quantified var
271	(consequent does NOT guard). A conjunction/disjunction/negation/atom body -> "no
272	toplevel implication". A guarded ∀ recurses into antecedent and consequent
273	(finds nested failures; the reported subformula is the inner failing quantifier).
274	∃ takes a conjunctive guard (`Ex x #i. A(x)@#i` is fine); an ∃ with unguarded
275	vars is "unguarded variable(s)".
276	
277	<sub>/<whole> use the multi-line formula printer (pp_formula_wrapped): a small
278	HughesPJ-style engine at page width ~72 (single line fits at total col 72, breaks
279	by 74). Rules (calibrated r3_gw/r3_qm/r3_and):
280	  - atom -> single-line text (never breaks); connective operands always parens'd.
281	  - binary op `(a) OP (b)`: breaks after OP; the right operand hangs at the
282	    connective's START COLUMN (the column just after its enclosing `(`).
283	  - quantifier `Q vars. body`: breaks after the `.`; body hangs at base+2
284	    (base = the quote indent 6, so body at col 8).
285	The formula is embedded as `      "..."` (quote col 6, formula col 7). When a
286	formula fits on one line the printer is byte-identical to the single-line
287	`pp_formula`, so narrow-formula fixtures are unaffected.
288	
289	### Nat Sorts
290	Trigger: var used in %+ (nat) context not of sort nat. Entries joined "\n  \n":
291	    "  y in term (y%+z) must be of sort nat" (nat-sort inference - structural only, gap).
292	
293	### Subterm Convergence Warning
294	Trigger: user equation l=r not subterm convergent. Body:
295	    "  User-defined equations must be convergent and have the finite variant property. The following equations are not subterm convergent. If you are sure that the set of equations is nevertheless convergent and has the finite variant property, you can ignore this warning and continue \n\n" + eqs + "\n   \n For more information, please refer to the manual : https://tamarin-prover.com/manual/master/book/010_modeling-issues.html "
296	each eq "    {lhsPP} = {rhsPP}" (subterm decision + printer partial - gap).
297	
298	## Term pretty-printer (observed)
299	- Var: sortprefix + base + ("."+idx if idx>0). Fresh `~`, Pub `$`, Nat `%`,
300	  Node `#`, Msg/Untagged ``.
301	- Pair / App("pair",[a,b]): `<a, b, ...>` (right-nested pairs flattened).
302	- App(f,args): `f(a, b)` (nullary -> `f`).
303	- PubLit s: `'s'`.
304	- BinOp: Exp `a^b` (no outer parens); Mult `(a*b)`; Union `(a++b)`;
305	  Xor `(a(+)b)` U+2295 AC-sorted; NatPlus `(a%+b)`.
306	- NatOne -> `%1`.
307	- Fact: [`!` if persistent] + name + "(" + (" "+args.join(", ")+" )" if args else " )").
308	
309	## Rule pretty-printer (observed; round2)
310	Used by Left/Right rule and Multiplication restriction. Base form (single line
311	when short):
312	    "rule (modulo E) NAME:\n   [ prems ] <arrow> [ concls ]"
313	- 3-space indent before the fact-list line; fact lists `[ f1, f2 ]`, `[ ]` empty.
314	- <arrow> = `-->` (no actions) or `--[ acts ]->` (actions present).
315	- `(modulo E)` (the AST modulo field, default "E").
316	- When embedded in a warning the whole 2-line block is indented (4 for
317	  Left/Right and Multiplication, giving 4 and 4+3=7 spaces).
318	GAP: long rules wrap - the arrow drops to its own line indented 6 and the
319	conclusions to indent 7 (probe mul_terms). Byte-parity fixtures use short rules.
320	
321	## Formula variable identity (round2 fix)
322	For free-variable detection (Formula terms) and guardedness the oracle treats a
323	quantified message variable and its occurrences as ONE variable even when the
324	parsed AST tags them with different-but-compatible sorts (Msg vs the Untagged
325	default; a temporal written `@ i` vs a quantifier `#i`). Matching on the full
326	sort tag produced spurious "Formula terms"/" Formula guardedness" reports on
327	round2/exists_trace_reuse (the integration bug). FIX: bind/compare formula
328	variables by NAME only. All prior fixtures (p05, p21, f_subterm) use consistent
329	sorts and are unaffected.
330	
331	## Round 3 (Unit C) - closed and residual
332	
333	CLOSED with byte-parity fixtures:
334	- Formula terms FULL coverage: reducible functions + de Bruijn `Bound N`, free
335	  vars inside terms, no-dedup source order, fillSep(69) list wrap, multi-lemma
336	  `\n  \n` separator (`formula_terms_reducible` / `check_theory_with_reducible`).
337	- Fact capitalization issues topic.
338	- Lemma-sourced fact arity/multiplicity (raw Haskell `Fact {..}` render).
339	- Fresh-const list wrapping; multi-group public-names separator.
340	- Guardedness: nested-subformula selection, the "universal quantifier without
341	  toplevel implication" mode, and the multi-line formula printer (implies-break
342	  and And-break byte-exact).
343	
344	## Out-of-scope / known gaps
345	- Message Derivation Checks & Derivation Checks: computed by Maude after
346	  translation; not derivable from the AST. Not produced by check_theory.
347	- AC operator argument ordering (xor/multiset) needs the term ordering; the raw
348	  Formula-terms show of BinOp/AlgApp operators is a best-effort name guess.
349	- RULE-PRINTER wide wrapping (RESIDUAL, item 4b): a wide rule wraps to a
350	  multi-line form - `[` / facts-fillSep / `]` for a broken fact list, `-->` on
351	  its own line (indent 6), header at 4 - and in the Multiplication context is
352	  co-printed with the out-of-scope Maude derivation reprint. The single-line rule
353	  printer stays byte-exact for short rules (all current fixtures). Structure
354	  recorded from r3_rulewrap; a dedicated rule-layout engine is future work.
355	- Guardedness ALGORITHM depth: ∃ failure sub-modes and exotic ∀ bodies beyond the
356	  probed cases; the formula printer's deeply-nested-quantifier hang is
357	  extrapolated (calibrated for outer-quantifier + binary-op breaks).
358	- Nat-sort inference / subterm-convergence decision: structural scaffolding only.

1	# Round 2 report — wellformedness clean-room
2	
3	Scope: close the five (six) warning topics the oracle emits that `check_theory()`
4	did not, plus the reported `exists_trace_reuse` false positive. All behavior was
5	re-derived by probing `oracle/wf_oracle.sh` (diff cases via `--diff`); no
6	tamarin-rs source was read.
7	
8	## Topics closed (byte-parity fixtures added)
9	
10	| Topic | Mode | Trigger | Report position |
11	|-------|------|---------|-----------------|
12	| `Fresh public constants` | both | `~'foo'` literal in a rule | #2 (after Unbound, before public-names) |
13	| `Reserved prefixes` | diff only | fact name starts `DiffIntr`/`DiffProto` | after `Reserved names` |
14	| `Left rule` / `Right rule` | diff only | explicit `left`/`right` != parent projection | after lhs-not-rhs, before Formula terms |
15	| `Lemma annotations` | both | `[reuse]` + `exists-trace` lemma | after Formula guardedness |
16	| `Multiplication restriction of rules` | both | `*` term in a rule's conclusions | after Lemma annotations, before Nat Sorts |
17	
18	Left rule and Right rule are one check (`diff_left_right`): the left projection is
19	tested first and, if inconsistent, the right is not reported for that rule
20	(observed via `diff_both`). All three diff topics are gated on `theory.is_diff`
21	(silent otherwise — confirmed by running the diff fixtures without `--diff`).
22	
23	## False positive fixed
24	
25	`round2/exists_trace_reuse` made the old code emit `Formula terms` +
26	` Formula guardedness`. Root cause: formula free-variable / guardedness matching
27	keyed on `(name, sort)`, so a quantified message var and its occurrence tagged
28	with different-but-compatible sorts (Msg vs the Untagged default; `@ i` vs `#i`)
29	looked distinct. Fix: bind and compare formula variables by NAME only. The
30	oracle emits only `Lemma annotations` for this input; `check_theory` now matches.
31	Prior fixtures (p05, p21, f_subterm) use consistent sorts and are unaffected.
32	
33	## New tests (all green: 37 + 1)
34	
35	Six byte-parity fixtures captured live from the oracle
36	(`tests/fixtures/r2_*.txt`) with matching hand-built ASTs:
37	`lemma_annotations_exists_trace_reuse`, `fresh_public_constant_literal`,
38	`multiplication_restriction_in_conclusion`, `diff_left_rule_inconsistent`,
39	`diff_right_rule_inconsistent`, `reserved_prefix_diff_only`. Plus
40	`diff_left_right_consistent_is_silent`, two ordering tests
41	(`fresh_public_constants_before_public_names`,
42	`lemma_annotations_between_guardedness_and_nat`), a non-diff silence assertion,
43	and a topics-set regression guard on the false positive. All six fixtures were
44	re-verified against the live oracle byte-for-byte.
45	
46	## New infrastructure
47	
48	`pretty::pp_rule` / `pp_fact_list` (rule printer: `rule (modulo E) N:\n   [..] <arrow> [..]`);
49	`checks::fill_words` (Wadler `fillSep` at measured width 69 for the Reserved
50	prefixes header); `indent_block`; diff term/fact projection.
51	
52	## Remaining gaps (documented in BEHAVIOR.md)
53	
54	- Rule-printer line wrapping for long rules (arrow drops to its own line) — fixtures use short rules.
55	- Multiplication: the "After replacing reducible symbols in lhs" rule is rendered
56	  equal to the original (correct only when the LHS has no reducible symbols); the
57	  alternate `Variables that occur only in rhs` failure mode is not implemented;
58	  the co-emitted Maude `Message Derivation Checks` block stays out of scope.
59	- Reserved prefixes: header word-wrap is reproduced (width 69), but the
60	  `(ProtoFact ...)` line is a raw Haskell tuple Show and multi-rule joining is
61	  unprobed; fresh-public-constant list wrapping (4-space continuation) is not
62	  reproduced — fixtures keep lists short.
63	- Diff mode merges `Fact arity`/`Fact multiplicity` into a single `Fact usage`
64	  topic (observed); `check_theory` still emits the two non-diff topics in diff
65	  mode. Out of the requested scope; noted for a future round.

1	//! wf-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover wellformedness
2	//! checker, derived purely from black-box oracle behavior. See
3	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the inferred behavioral spec.
4	
5	pub mod ast;
6	pub mod checks;
7	pub mod formula;
8	pub mod pretty;
9	pub mod report;
10	
11	pub use ast::*;
12	pub use report::{
13	    insert_wf_before, render_report, topics, underline_topic, WfError, WfReport, SUCCESS_LINE,
14	};
15	
16	/// Topics that follow the public-names report in the canonical order; the
17	/// public-names report is inserted immediately before the first of these that
18	/// is present in the report. (Unbound variables and Fresh public constants
19	/// precede the insertion point and are therefore NOT anchors.)
20	pub fn after_public_names_topics() -> Vec<&'static str> {
21	    vec![
22	        checks::T_SORTS,
23	        checks::T_RESERVED,
24	        checks::T_RESERVED_PREFIX,
25	        checks::T_FR,
26	        checks::T_SPECIAL,
27	        checks::T_FACT_CAP,
28	        checks::T_ARITY,
29	        checks::T_MULT,
30	        checks::T_LHSRHS,
31	        checks::T_LEFT,
32	        checks::T_RIGHT,
33	        checks::T_FORMULA_TERMS,
34	        checks::T_GUARD,
35	        checks::T_LEMMA_ANNOT,
36	        checks::T_MULRESTRICT,
37	        checks::T_NAT,
38	        checks::T_SUBTERM,
39	    ]
40	}
41	
42	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order. The "Formula terms" check runs
43	/// in free-variable-only mode; for reducible-function coverage use
44	/// [`check_theory_with_reducible`].
45	pub fn check_theory(thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
46	    check_theory_with_reducible(thy, &std::collections::BTreeSet::new())
47	}
48	
49	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order, passing a caller-computed set
50	/// of reducible function-symbol names to the "Formula terms" check (theories
51	/// with `equations:` make some user functions reducible; the caller derives
52	/// that set - typically from the equation theory / Maude - and this pipeline
53	/// only consumes it).
54	pub fn check_theory_with_reducible(
55	    thy: &Theory,
56	    reducible: &std::collections::BTreeSet<String>,
57	) -> WfReport {
58	    let mut report: WfReport = Vec::new();
59	    // Base pipeline (public names inserted afterwards at its anchor point).
60	    report.extend(checks::unbound_variables(thy)); // 1
61	    report.extend(checks::fresh_public_constants(thy)); // 2
62	    report.extend(checks::mismatching_sorts(thy)); // 4
63	    report.extend(checks::reserved_names(thy)); // 5
64	    report.extend(checks::reserved_prefixes(thy)); // 6 (diff mode only)
65	    report.extend(checks::fr_facts(thy)); // 7
66	    report.extend(checks::special_facts(thy)); // 8
67	    report.extend(checks::fact_capitalization(thy)); // 8b (precedes arity)
68	    report.extend(checks::fact_arity(thy)); // 9
69	    report.extend(checks::fact_multiplicity(thy)); // 10
70	    report.extend(checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs(thy)); // 11
71	    report.extend(checks::diff_left_right(thy)); // 12, 13 (diff mode only)
72	    report.extend(checks::formula_terms_reducible(thy, reducible)); // 14
73	    report.extend(checks::formula_guardedness(thy)); // 15
74	    report.extend(checks::lemma_annotations(thy)); // 16
75	    report.extend(checks::multiplication_restriction(thy)); // 17
76	    report.extend(checks::nat_sorts(thy)); // 18
77	    report.extend(checks::subterm_convergence(thy)); // 19
78	
79	    let pn = checks::public_names_report(thy); // 3
80	    let anchors = after_public_names_topics();
81	    insert_wf_before(&mut report, pn, &anchors);
82	    report
83	}
84	
85	/// Secondary entry point: report any of `lemma_names` that are not declared in
86	/// the theory. NOTE: this entry point's exact render string could not be
87	/// observed through the file oracle; the set-difference logic is verified but
88	/// the message text is a documented gap (see BEHAVIOR.md).
89	pub fn check_if_lemmas_in_theory(lemma_names: &[String], thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
90	    let present = checks::theory_lemma_names(thy);
91	    let mut out = Vec::new();
92	    for name in lemma_names {
93	        if !present.contains(name) {
94	            out.push(WfError::new(
95	                "Lemmas",
96	                format!("lemma `{}' referenced but not present in theory", name),
97	            ));
98	        }
99	    }
100	    out
101	}
102	
103	// Re-export the public secondary entry points named in required_api.md.
104	pub use checks::{
105	    fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs, formula_terms, formula_terms_reducible, public_names_report,
106	    public_names_report_from_pairs,
107	};

  - `universal quantifier without toplevel implication`

DECISION PROCEDURE (pre-order; first failure reported, antecedent before
consequent, left before right - probes gr_both/gr_sib):
  - `∀ vars. body`: body MUST be a top-level implication `guard ==> rest`;
    otherwise "without toplevel implication" (bare atom, conjunction,
    disjunction, negation, iff ALL fail this way - gu_bareatom/gu_conj/gu_disj/
    gu_neg/gu_iff). If it is `guard ==> rest`, every quantified var must be in
    the guard set, else "unguarded variable(s)"; then recurse into guard then
    rest.
  - `∃ vars. body`: every quantified var must be in the guard set of `body`,
    else "unguarded variable(s)" (a non-conjunction body - disjunction/
    implication/negation - guards nothing: ge_disj/ge_impl/ge_neg); then recurse
    into body. A bare action atom or a conjunction of them is a valid guard
    (ge_bareact/ge_conj_ok).
  - GUARD SET of a formula = the (name, sort-class) keys of variables occurring
    in ACTION atoms reachable through CONJUNCTIONS only. Disjunction, negation,
    implication, equality/`<`/`⋖`/`⊏`/`last` atoms, and nested quantifiers
    contribute NO guards (gx_disj_ant/gx_neg_ant/gx_eq_ant/gx_less/gx_last/
    gx_quant_g). An action atom guards all its fact-arg vars AND its temporal
    (gx_pair). Guard matching is sort-aware (syn_pubsuffix: pub `$x` is NOT
    guarded by a msg `x` in `A(x)`). This is the only semantic change from the
    round-3 heuristic (which recursed guards through Or/Not/quantifiers).
  - `¬`, `∧`, `∨`, `⇒`, `⇔` at the top: recurse into operands (left first).
  - Any non-quantified formula: no failure (gt_noquant/gt_topconj).

MULTI-LINE FORMULA PRINTER (pp_formula_wrapped, col-relative; round4 fix).
Page width 72 (single line fits at total col 72, breaks by 74). The formula is

# Round 4 report — wellformedness clean-room (Unit C)

Scope: close the two gaps that kept `check_theory` from fully replacing
post-elaboration wellformedness checking. All behavior re-derived by probing
`oracle/wf_oracle.sh`; no tamarin-rs / Haskell source read. Round-4 probes are in
`scratchpad/probes4`, logged in `QUERIES.log`, spec'd in `BEHAVIOR.md`.

## GAP 1 — sort-aware quantifier binding

The wrong-form-terms check bound quantifiers by NAME only (round-2), which
over-binds. The reference distinguishes variable SORTS: a temporal binder `#x`
does not bind a message use `x` (`All #x. (A(x) @ #x) ==> (#x = #x)` →
`Formula terms: \`Free x'`), and a message binder `x` does not bind a node use
`#x` (→ `Free #x`). Fix: the de Bruijn binder stack now carries `(name, sort
class)` and matches on both. Sort classes collapse `{Untagged, Msg}` (which is
what the round-2 false positive actually needed) but keep Pub/Fresh/Node/Nat
distinct. De Bruijn indexing still counts every quantified var, so
`All x #x. … h(x)` → `h(Bound 1)` (probe g1_dbsort).

Probing the sort-annotation interaction surfaced a **previously-unimplemented
topic, `Quantifier sorts`**: quantifying over a public or fresh variable is
